title: Dense Smoked Middle-Eastern Smoked Haddock Umm Ali
description: A rich and satisfying umm ali featuring smoked haddock, sweet corn, and crunchy binchotan charcoal, all wrapped in a corn husk. This diabetic-friendly dessert is perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Rinse the smoked haddock under cold water, pat dry with paper towels, and cut into small pieces.
In a large bowl, mix together the coconut milk, honey, cinnamon, cardamom, and salt.
Add the binchotan charcoal to the bowl and stir to combine.
Add the corn kernels to the bowl and stir to combine.
Add the smoked haddock to the bowl and stir to combine.
Pour the mixture into the corn husks, dividing it evenly among the four wraps.
Fold the top of each wrap over the filling and secure with a toothpick.
Place the wraps on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15 minutes, or until the filling is heated through and the corn husks are tender.
While the wraps are baking, mix together the Greek yogurt and a pinch of salt in a small bowl.
Once the wraps are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Unwrap the desserts and top each one with a dollop of yogurt and a sprinkle of parsley.
